meaning: vt.重新建立,再设立,再制订,再开创
examples: To reinstitute the death penalty review (to the high court) does not mean we will abolish capital punishment.
His plan was to crack down on arrogance and excess and reinstitute frugality, according to executives close to Mr. Toyoda.
The prevailing westerly will reinstitute themselves along the new Equator, curling away from the Equator as they do today, and returning.
He would reinstitute the "death tax" (estate tax) with a top rate of 45%.
Mr. Weill was then asked if he meant that we should reinstitute Glass-Steagall.
